title: Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi
tagline: Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Sri Vidhya Rajagopalaswamy temple is a Vaishnava shrine located in the town of Mannargudi, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Sri Vidhya Rajagopalaswamy temple is a Vaishnava shrine located in the town of Mannargudi, Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Rajagopalaswamy, a form of Krishna and his consort Lakshmi, who is worshipped as Sri Sengamala Thayar. The temple is spread over an area of 9.3 ha (23 acres) and is an important Vaishnava shrines in India. The temple is called Champakaranya Kshetram, Dakshina Dwaraka (Southern Dvaraka) along with Guruvayoor by Hindus, Vanduvaraapathi and Vasudevapuri.

topPlaces.name: Mannargudi taluk
topPlaces.description: Mannargudi taluk is a taluk in Thiruvarur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Mannargudi.

topPlaces.name: Mannargudi division
topPlaces.description: Mannargudi division is a revenue division in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India. It comprises the taluks of Mannargudi, Needamangalam and Thiruthiraipoondi.

topPlaces.name: Mannargudi block
topPlaces.description: Mannargudi block is a revenue block in the Mannargudi taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 51 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Mallinathaswamy Jain Temple, Mannargudi
topPlaces.description: Mallinathaswamy Jain Temple (மன்னார்குடி மல்லிநாதசுவாமி ஜினாலயம்) is a Jain temple dedicated to the deity Jain, located in Mannargudi, an ancient town in the erstwhile Chola Empire in Tiruvarur District of Tamil Nadu, India. Mallinathaswamy or Māllīnātha is the 19th Tirthankara of the Jain faith.

topPlaces.name: Mannargudi
topPlaces.description: Mannargudi (Tamil pronunciation: [mannaːrgɯɖi]) is a town in Thiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the headquarters of the Mannargudi taluk.

topPlaces.name: Kunniyur
topPlaces.description: Kunniyur is a village in Thiruvarur district in, Tamil Nadu, India. It is near the town of Mannargudi.

topPlaces.name: Pookkollai
topPlaces.description: Pookkollai is a town in the southern area of Mannargudi, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is relatively a bigger area in the peravurani represented by two ward presidents in the panchayat body.

topPlaces.name: Pamani Naganathar Temple
topPlaces.description: Pamani Naganathar Temple(பாமணி நாகநாதர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Pamani in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Naganathar.

Mannargudi division is a revenue division in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India. It comprises the taluks of Mannargudi, Needamangalam and Thiruthiraipoondi.
Mannargudi block is a revenue block in the Mannargudi taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 51 panchayat villages.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (84 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi?
faq.a: Stays in Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rajagopalaswamy Temple, Mannargudi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mannargudi taluk, Mannargudi division, Mannargudi block, Mallinathaswamy Jain Temple, Mannargudi, Mannargudi.
